Improved workflow is a benefit for business rather than for customers. businesses. Retrieved August 2, 2016, from http://readwrite.com/2010/03/01/bundled- pricing-benefit-cusotmers-businesses/
sources: SOURCE: Cameron, C. (2010, March 1). How bundled pricing can benefit both customers and

questions.question: Jill only buys Natural Glow cosmetics and will go from store to store until she finds them. Jill is in the brand __________ stage of brand loyalty.
questions.explanation: Insistence. Brand insistence has been achieved when a consumer refuses alternative products and searches exclusively for the desired merchandise. In this case, Jill insists upon Natural Glow cosmetics and makes sure she buys them over any other brand of cosmetics. Brand evangelism occurs when you are an advocate for a brand, convincing others that the brand is the best. In this case, there is no indication that Jill is a brand evangelist for Natural Glow. Brand preference occurs when you prefer a brand based on previous experience with it and will select it over competitors when the option is available. In this case, Jill is beyond the level of brand preference because she only wants Natural Glow, even when it is not available. Brand recognition means that the consumer is aware of and can identify a brand. Jill is beyond this point with Natural Glow. http://tofurious.com/marketing-strategy/the-4-degrees-of-brand-loyalty/
sources: SOURCE: Chan, L. (2013, April 30). The 4 degrees of brand loyalty. Retrieved August 3, 2016, from

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of using customer voice to build your brand:

options.A: Blocking all negative social media posts that customers write about your brand
options.B: Posting positive customer reviews prominently on your website
options.C: Promoting the best-selling products that you already know customers love
options.D: Letting customers spread the word about your brand to their friends and family

questions.explanation: Posting positive customer reviews prominently on your website. Customer feedback is a great way to promote your brand as positive and trustworthy. You should prominently display your best customer reviews on your website so prospective customers will develop a positive attitude toward your brand. Blocking all negative social media posts that customers might write about your brand will make you seem untrustworthy and dishonest. It is better to address these complaints. Promoting the best-selling products that you already know customers love does not involve using customer voice. Finally, letting customers spread the word about your brand is not actively using customer voice to promote or build your brand. August 3, 2016, from http://www.jeffbullas.com/2016/01/12/7-tips-market-brand-using- customer-reviews/ 2017 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 30
sources: SOURCE: Roy, A. (2016, Jan. 12). 7 tips to market your brand using customer reviews. Retrieved

questions.question: A company's strategy, culture, and communication combine to form brand
questions.explanation: Identity. Brand identity is the culmination of what a company wants to be—what it says about who it is. Strategy, culture, and communication all contribute to brand identity. Brand touchpoints are any instances in which the customers encounter the brand, such as a promotional message or a company website. Brand cues are messages that affect buying behavior in regard to a brand, such as a certain word that makes a customer think of a product. A brand promise is a commitment made by a brand to its customers about what it intends to deliver. http://www.investopedia.com/terms/b/brand-identity.asp
sources: SOURCE: Investopedia. (2016). Brand identity. Retrieved August 2, 2016, from

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a customer touchpoint that is important during the purchase stage:
questions.explanation: A retail store. Touchpoints are any interactions a business has with customers. Touchpoints give customers the opportunity to form an opinion of the brand. When a customer is making a purchase, a retail store is a touchpoint that a customer might encounter. Product reviews are more likely to be a factor during the pre-purchase stage. Customer service teams are likely to be a touchpoint during the post- purchase phase. A charity sponsorship is more likely to be important during the pre-purchase stage. from https://www.surveymonkey.com/mp/identify-customer-touchpoints/
sources: SOURCE: SurveyMonkey. (2016). How to identify customer touchpoints. Retrieved August 3, 2016,

questions.question: You see a billboard advertising beef from the Cattlemen's Beef Board. This is an example of __________ product promotion.
questions.explanation: Primary. Primary product promotion aims to stimulate demand, or desire, for an entire class of goods or services. The emphasis is on the product and its uses, rather than on a particular brand. When you see an advertisement for beef from the Cattlemen's Beef Board, you are receiving a primary promotional message. Secondary, or selective, product promotion is used to stimulate demand for a specific brand of a product. Public-service promotions inform customers about noncontroversial issues that are in the public's best interest. Public-relations promotion is a type of institutional promotion created to deal with controversial public issues that are related to a company or its products.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-PR-004—Know Your Options (Types of Promotion)

questions.question: Will wants to open a food truck business, but he lives in a city where many other food trucks already operate. Will should

options.A: set up meetings with potential customers to explain his business.
options.B: run a local radio ad that explains why his food truck is different.
options.C: perform charitable activities so he will receive positive publicity.

questions.explanation: Run a local radio ad that explains why his food truck is different. Advertisements are an effective promotional strategy when a product needs to be differentiated from its competitors, as well as when a product is in its introductory stage. Therefore, a radio ad that focuses on differentiating Will's food truck from others is an appropriate tactic. Setting up meetings with potential customers to explain his business is not an effective strategy for Will because personal selling is not generally used for convenience goods such as food. Will would not be able to meet with enough customers to make his business profitable. Performing charitable activities to receive good publicity would be more effective when Will has already established his brand. It is hard to generate publicity during the introductory stage. Finally, a merchandising allowance is a sales promotion that is used when promoting to intermediary businesses, rather than directly to consumers, as is the case with Will's food truck.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-PR-001—Spread the Word (Nature of Promotional Mix)

questions.question: Which of the following examples depicts an advantage of using digital advertising:

options.A: Conversion rates of banner ads are more difficult to measure than in print.
options.B: Ethan uses ad-blocking software so he can stream videos commercial-free.
options.C: Viewers must interact with a video ad before continuing to a website.
options.D: Digital ads are more expensive than television advertisements.

questions.explanation: Viewers must interact with a video ad before continuing to a website. One of the biggest challenges facing any advertisement is engagement—keeping the viewers' attention and making sure they are receiving the message. It is easy for viewers to ignore video ads, either digital or traditional, but if they are forced to interact with the ad in some way, they are more likely to pay attention to the ad and take in its message. Ad-blocking software is a disadvantage to digital advertising, rather than an advantage. Conversion rates are easier to measure online than in print. Finally, digital ads are generally cheaper than television advertisements. Western, Cengage Learning. 2017 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 31
sources: SOURCE: Kurtz, D. (2013). Contemporary marketing: 2013 edition (pp. 516, 534). Mason, OH: South-

questions.question: Which of the following advertisements is most likely to violate promotional regulations:

options.A: A toy commercial uses bright colors and a catchy jingle.
options.B: A food advertisement depicts a child using a stove unsupervised.
options.C: An email newsletter includes an opt-out link at the bottom.
options.D: The people in a prescription drug commercial are designated as actors.

questions.explanation: A food advertisement depicts a child using a stove unsupervised. Advertisements that are unfair or cause injury to consumers can violate consumer protection regulations. An advertisement that shows a child using the stove unsupervised can be dangerous to children who might see the advertisement and think it's okay for them to use the stove by themselves. A toy commercial that uses bright colors and a catchy jingle would not necessarily violate any regulation. Email newsletters should always include an opt-out link so that they don't violate regulation. Finally, a prescription drug commercial should clarify that the people in it are actors, rather than actual users of the drug. present. Retrieved August 16, 2016, from https://www.ftc.gov/sites/default/files/documents/public_statements/advertising-kids-and-ftc- regulatory-retrospective-advises-present/040802adstokids.pdf

questions.question: A local pizza place gives you a magnet that includes its logo, website, and phone number. This is an example of
questions.explanation: Specialty media. Specialty media is a type of “other” media that features a business's logo, is useful, and is given away freely. A magnet featuring a business's logo, website, and phone number is considered specialty media. Directory advertising includes promotional messages found in directories of a general nature such as the Yellow Pages or in specialized listings of companies in an industry. Product placement is a type of “other” media that uses products as props in movies, television shows, and computer games. A spectacular is a type of out-of-home advertisement built to order and often designed with neon lights or moving parts.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-PR-003—Ad-Quipping Your Business (Types of Advertising Media)

questions.question: If a retailer is trying to clear its inventory of a particular product, which of the following would be the most effective type of sales promotion:

options.A: A sweepstakes for a new car
options.B: A contest that rewards the 100th customer
options.C: A booth at a trade show

questions.explanation: A Buy-One-Get-One-Free coupon. Sales promotions are intended to stimulate customer purchases, usually in the short-term. A Buy-One-Get-One-Free coupon helps entice customers to buy a product that they might not normally buy. If the retailer is trying to get rid of merchandise, a coupon focused on that product would help to promote it. A contest that rewards the store's 100th customer, a booth at a trade show, and a sweepstakes for a new car would not necessarily promote the particular product in question, though they are all forms of sales promotion. Western, Cengage Learning.
sources: SOURCE: Kurtz, D. (2013). Contemporary marketing: 2013 edition (p. 501). Mason, OH: South-

questions.question: Which of the following is an important component of a business's press kit:
questions.explanation: Biographies of key company figures. A press kit is a package of information about a company, whether physical or digital, that is sent out to reporters in hopes of generating interest about that company. The press kit should be a “one-stop shop” of all information a reporter could need to write about the company. This includes biographies of key figures in the company, such as the CEO, COO, etc. Promotional messages, interviews, and customer testimonials are not necessarily important components of a press kit. http://www.inc.com/guides/2010/09/how-to-put-together-a-press-kit.html
sources: SOURCE: Donnelly, T. How to put together a press kit. Retrieved August 4, 2016, from

questions.question: What is the first requirement for writing successful advertising copy?
questions.explanation: Grab attention. If an ad's copy does not grab a reader's attention, it will never be seen, read, or understood. Therefore, a piece of copy must first and foremost attract attention. Being informative, persuading readers, and demanding action are all important but are not the first priorities. Attention must be captured first before any of these requirements can be achieved. http://www.bizmove.com/marketing/m2j3.htm 2017 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 32
sources: SOURCE: BizMove. (n.d.) Writing advertising ads. Retrieved August 5, 2016, from

questions.question: A furniture store released an ad in the weekly paper, but readers complained that it was too busy and difficult to know where to focus. The advertisement should have included more
questions.explanation: White space. White space is essential because it separates images on the page, keeps the page from looking cluttered and messy, and makes the advertisement clearer. White space could help the furniture store's ad to look less busy. More fonts would probably make the ad look busier and would not necessarily help the readers know where to focus. Logos and graphics would not necessarily help the ad to be less busy. 2016, from http://www.serif.com/blog/how-important-is-white-space-in-print-design/
sources: SOURCE: Mauloni, M. (2013, Jan 7). How important is white space in print design? Retrieved August 3,

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a one-way public-relations activity that could be used to distribute company information directly to the target audience:
questions.explanation: Newsletters. Newsletters are one-way communications from the company to its target audience that contain important information about the company. They can be sent via email or through regular mail. Search engine optimization is a tactic used to boost an organization's ability to be discovered through search engine tools. It is not a direct way of distributing information to the target audience; rather, it is a strategy that can be employed in many types of PR content. Press conferences and social media are not one-way public relations activities; rather, they are interactive. http://www.buzzle.com/articles/public-relations-tools.html
sources: SOURCE: Rampur, S. (2011, Sept 27). Tools of public relations. Retrieved August 4, 2016, from

questions.question: Cozy Cup Coffee moved into a new location in a residential area. Many of the neighbors were not thrilled about the construction noise and increased traffic. Cozy Cup Coffee decided to give out free cups of coffee to the neighbors every morning. This is an example of

options.A: why residential areas are not appropriate targets for public relations activities.
options.B: how company shareholders are the most important audience of public relations.
options.C: why company vendors get in the way of community relations.
options.D: how companies can target the community with public relations efforts.

questions.explanation: How companies can target the community with public relations efforts. One potential audience of public relations activities is the community. Businesses cannot avoid interacting with the community, so they should strive to maintain a good relationship. Cozy Cup Coffee realizes that its relationship with the community is tenuous, so it engages in a public relations effort by giving out free cups of coffee. This example does not depict how company shareholders are the most important audience of public relations, why company vendors get in the way of community relations, or that residential areas are not appropriate targets for public relations activities. http://www.evancarmichael.com/library/martin-cohn/The-Importance-of-Community- Relations.html
sources: SOURCE: Cohn, M. (n.d.) The importance of community relations. Retrieved August 5, 2016, from

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a tangible product:
questions.explanation: A pizza. Pizza is a tangible product because it can be detected by the senses. Tangible products can be touched, smelled, tasted, seen, or heard. An insurance policy, cooking lessons, and a haircut are all intangible products because they are services you could pay someone else to perform. You cannot detect intangible objects through the senses.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-SE-117—Sell Away (The Nature and Scope of Selling)

questions.question: Joaquin, a car salesman, is sure to send out a personalized thank-you note every time he makes a sale. Which key to customer service is he demonstrating?
questions.explanation: Keeping in touch. Good salespeople recognize that keeping in contact with customers is one of the best ways to maintain their partnerships. Sometimes this involves sending out a thank-you note, as in Joaquin's case. Sometimes this can be as simple as a follow-up phone call, or for major clients, it may even involve a personal visit. Soliciting feedback, being prompt, and having a good attitude are all important keys to customer service, but Joaquin is not demonstrating them in this example. 2017 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 33
sources: SOURCE: LAP-SE-130—Go Beyond the Sale (Customer Service in Selling)

questions.question: Elizabeth is looking for a new laptop for college. She visits a technology store, finds a computer that she likes, and asks the salesperson about the warranty on the laptop. Elizabeth is asking about the store's __________ policy.
questions.explanation: Guarantee. These types of policies are guarantees of quality or product performance and often include warranties which cover a certain period of time or specific parts of the product. Repair and maintenance policies are policies that offer continued care of a product for a specified or indefinite amount of time. Companies who offer potentially dangerous products or services (parasailing or bungee jumping, for example) may have a liability policy that requires salespeople to obtain a liability waiver from the customer. These waivers release the company from responsibility for something that might go wrong. Return and adjustment policies govern the return of goods, including how and when customers can return products.

questions.question: Which of the following sales actions is considered illegal:

options.A: A salesperson ignores his/her coworker's unethical actions.
options.B: An employer puts too much pressure on his/her sales employees.
options.C: A salesperson says something untrue that damages a competitor's business.

questions.explanation: A salesperson says something untrue that damages a competitor's business. When you say something untrue that damages a person's or business's reputation, that's considered slander, which is illegal. An employer putting too much pressure on his/her sales employees and a salesperson ignoring his/her coworker's misbehavior are both considered unethical actions, but they aren't illegal. A salesperson taking a client out to dinner is not typically considered unethical or illegal, although if a salesperson isn't careful, s/he could cross over into the legal issue of bribery. likeness. sources: SOURCE: US Legal. (n.d.). False advertising, trade defamation, and misappropriation of a name or

questions.question: Which of the following is a true statement regarding the use of product information for selling:

options.A: It is impossible to give customers too much product information.
options.B: Salespeople should not rely on selling skills alone.
options.C: It is not necessary to acquire information about the industry as a whole.
options.D: Salespeople should rely on technical language when sharing product information.

questions.explanation: Salespeople should not rely on selling skills alone. It is important to acquire product information for answering customers' questions, demonstrating how the product meets their needs, etc. Selling skills alone will not result in sales success. Sometimes, it is possible to overwhelm customers by giving them too much information or by using too much technical jargon. It is important for salespeople to acquire industry information as well as product information.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-SE-131—Get Informed (Acquiring Product Information for Use in Selling)

questions.question: Which of the following statements describes features:

options.A: They answer the question, "What's in it for me?"
options.B: They can be obvious, exclusive, or hidden.
options.C: They are the advantages a customer wants from a product.

questions.explanation: They are physical characteristics. A feature is a physical characteristic or quality of a product. It is something the customer can touch, feel, smell, see, or measure. It helps to describe the product. In short, a feature answers the question, “What is it?” Benefits, not features, can be obvious, exclusive, or hidden. Benefits are the advantages a customer wants from a product, and they answer the question, “What's in it for me?”
sources: SOURCE: LAP-SE-113—Find Features, Boost Benefits (Feature-Benefit Selling)

questions.question: In what stage of the selling process do salespeople handle objections?
questions.explanation: Reaching closure. Closing a sale often has two distinct parts or steps—overcoming objections and getting the order. Clearing up objections can serve as a chance for the salesperson to demonstrate his/her knowledge and further solidify the product's benefits to the customer. Prescribing solutions to a customer's needs involves making product suggestions based on a customer's specific situation, and occurs after the salesperson has determined the customer's needs through questioning. Reaffirming the buyer-seller relationship occurs after a sale has been made, when the salesperson reassures the customer that they have made a good purchase decision. Preparing to sell is the very first phase of the selling process, and involves preliminary research and planning the sales presentation. 2017 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 34
sources: SOURCE: LAP-SE-126—Set Your Sales (The Selling Process)

questions.question: A grocery store chain recently started stocking a new brand of ice cream after noticing the brand's popularity at competing grocery stores. The grocery store chain's decision to stock the new ice cream brand was motivated by __________ factors.
questions.explanation: Environmental. Environmental factors drive people or organizations to make purchases driven by the current business, social, or government environments. Factors such as competition and consumer demand are considered environmental factors. Interpersonal factors like status, persuasiveness, and authority can influence whether or not an organization or person purchases a product or service. Organizational factors include influences like corporate culture, role expectations, and company reward systems. Individual factors, like reward valence, personal needs, and personal relevance, can also drive people or organizations to make purchases. 2016, from http://www.slideshare.net/cshekhar8/factors-influencing-industrial-buying-ism
sources: SOURCE: Singh, C. (2014, January 29). Factors influencing industrial buying. Retrieved August 17,
